description: CE owns a new service-request host package or feature slice that defines the core domain types and public interfaces for portal service requests
CE defines a `ServiceRequestExecutionProvider` interface for pluggable submission execution behavior
CE defines a `ServiceRequestFormBehaviorProvider` interface for pluggable form behavior
CE defines a `ServiceRequestVisibilityProvider` interface for pluggable visibility evaluation
CE defines a `ServiceRequestTemplateProvider` interface for starter template registration
CE optionally defines a `ServiceRequestAdminExtensionProvider` interface for provider-specific authoring panels
CE ships a built-in `ticket-only` execution provider
CE ships a built-in `basic` form-behavior provider
CE ships a built-in `all-authenticated-client-users` visibility provider
CE ships at least one starter template pack that creates normal editable service request drafts
Service-request entrypoints resolve providers by key through registries instead of hard-coded enterprise branching
EE can register additional providers through a single enterprise entrypoint without changing CE table shapes
The feature remains coherent and buildable in CE when EE provider registrations are absent
Migration creates a tenant-owned `service_request_definitions` table for mutable draft definitions
Definitions store portal-facing metadata including name, description, icon, and sort order
Definitions store an optional category reference used for portal grouping
Definitions store an optional `linked_service_id` reference to `service_catalog`
Definitions store provider keys for execution, form behavior, and visibility
Definitions store provider config blobs for execution, form behavior, and visibility
Definitions track draft, published, and archived lifecycle state
Definitions track created-by, updated-by, and publish-related audit timestamps needed for normal product behavior
Migration creates an immutable `service_request_definition_versions` table
Published versions snapshot portal metadata from the source definition
Published versions snapshot form schema and field presentation from the source definition
Published versions snapshot provider keys and provider config from the source definition
Version numbers increment monotonically per definition as new publishes occur
Publishing records the user and timestamp responsible for a given version
Definitions can create a new mutable draft based on the currently published version without mutating historical versions
Migration creates a tenant-owned `service_request_submissions` table for durable intake records
Submissions store the definition id and version id used when the request was submitted
Submissions store tenant, client, contact, and requester user ownership fields
Submissions store the normalized submitted payload captured from the published definition snapshot
Submissions store execution status and a concise execution error summary when downstream startup fails
Submissions store `created_ticket_id` when a ticket-backed provider succeeds
Submissions store `workflow_execution_id` or equivalent workflow reference when a workflow-backed provider succeeds
Submissions store created and updated timestamps for request-history presentation
Migration creates a `service_request_submission_attachments` table or equivalent attachment linkage structure
Attachment rows link a submission to persisted file or document storage references
Archiving a definition preserves its versions and historical submissions
Historical submissions remain readable even when linked service-catalog or category records later change names
MSP admins can navigate to a new Service Requests management area
The Service Requests list shows draft, published, and archived definitions
The Service Requests list supports creating a blank definition
The Service Requests list supports creating a definition from a template
Admins can duplicate an existing definition into a new draft
Admins can archive and unarchive definitions from the management surface
The editor has a basics section for name, description, icon, category, and sort order
The editor has a linkage section for optional service-catalog association
The editor has a form-authoring section for the request form
The editor has an execution section driven by the selected execution provider
The editor has a publish section that distinguishes current draft state from the last published version
Admins can save an incomplete draft without making it visible in the portal
Admins can preview the portal-facing service card before publishing
Admins can preview the rendered request form before publishing
Publishing validates the full definition and blocks publication when required configuration is missing or invalid
Publishing creates a new immutable version rather than editing the prior published state in place
Template instantiation creates a normal editable draft that is detached from the template source after creation
CE starter templates are presented as optional shortcuts rather than a required setup path
Admins can view submissions scoped to a specific definition from the management experience
Admins can open a submission detail view showing requester identity, submitted values, and downstream execution references
The CE basic form builder supports a short-text field type
The CE basic form builder supports a long-text field type
The CE basic form builder supports a dropdown/select field type
The CE basic form builder supports a checkbox field type
The CE basic form builder supports a date field type
The CE basic form builder supports a file-upload field type
Field configuration supports label and help text for each field
Field configuration supports marking fields as required
Field configuration supports static default values for supported field types
Select fields support admin-authored option lists
Admins can add new fields to the form builder
Admins can remove fields from the form builder
Admins can reorder fields in the form builder
Generated field keys remain stable across reorder and presentation-only edits
Publish validation rejects duplicate or invalid field keys in the basic builder
The basic builder stores a neutral schema representation on the draft definition
The published version stores the immutable form snapshot derived from the draft definition
The portal client shows a first-class `Request Services` navigation item
The Request Services landing page shows only published definitions
The Request Services landing page groups definitions by category when categories are configured
The Request Services landing page evaluates visibility rules before listing a definition to a portal user
Service cards display icon, title, and short description from the published definition
Opening a service card navigates to a request detail page for the published definition
The request detail page renders the form from the immutable published version snapshot rather than from the mutable draft
Static default values are applied when the portal request form first renders
Portal users can provide file uploads for definitions that include file-upload fields
Submit-time validation enforces required-field rules against the published version snapshot
Submit-time validation rejects direct portal submissions to definitions that are not currently visible to the requesting user
Submitting a request persists a durable submission row before provider execution begins
Successful submission shows a confirmation state containing a stable request identifier
Portal users can view a My Requests list scoped to their client ownership
The My Requests list shows request name, submission time, and current submission status
Portal users can open a request-detail page for one of their own submissions
Submission details render the submitted answers using the field presentation from the version snapshot
Submission details show uploaded attachment references when the submission included file uploads
Portal users cannot list or open submissions that belong to a different client/contact context
The `ticket-only` execution provider is selectable in the editor without requiring any EE code
The `ticket-only` provider lets admins configure default ticket routing values needed by the existing portal ticket creation path
The `ticket-only` provider maps request payload values into ticket title and ticket description/notes content
The `ticket-only` provider uses the existing client-portal contact/client resolution model instead of inventing a parallel access model
The `ticket-only` provider writes the created ticket id back onto the submission when ticket creation succeeds
The `ticket-only` provider marks the submission with execution failure while keeping the submission persisted when ticket creation fails
Confirmation and request-history views expose a linked ticket reference when a ticket-backed submission succeeds
Definitions can be published successfully without any linked service-catalog item
The linked-service picker reuses existing service-catalog search patterns rather than introducing a second catalog-search model
The editor shows the selected linked-service record clearly enough for admins to confirm what commercial service is associated
Publish validation rejects linked-service references that no longer resolve to a valid service-catalog row
Templates may prefill a linked service and ticket-mapping defaults while remaining fully editable before publish
EE registers a `workflow-only` execution provider through the enterprise provider entrypoint
EE registers a `ticket-plus-workflow` execution provider through the enterprise provider entrypoint
Workflow-backed provider configuration lets admins choose the workflow to run for a definition
Workflow-backed provider configuration lets admins map request submission data into workflow inputs
The `ticket-plus-workflow` provider can pass the created ticket id into workflow inputs alongside the submitted request data
Successful workflow-backed execution writes the resulting workflow reference back onto the submission record
Workflow startup failures leave the submission persisted and marked with execution failure
Portal request-history views can surface workflow references when the selected provider creates them
EE registers an advanced form-behavior provider through the enterprise provider entrypoint
The advanced form-behavior provider supports conditional show/hide rules for fields
The advanced form-behavior provider supports requester, client, or context-aware default values
Publish validation rejects invalid conditional-logic configuration in advanced form mode
